content.method: Cohort tracking study following training-program entrants through graduation and employment placement over 3-5 years, disaggregated by region and motivation (career-track vs. parent-enrollee); combine with salary-differential analysis comparing specialist versus generalist compensation trajectories post-reform.
content.question: Will the January 2025 teacher salary reform and rising special-education training enrollments translate into increased practicing specialist supply, particularly in underserved regions, or does the training pipeline overstate future workforce availability?
content.why_it_matters: Workforce expansion (T5) is politically the least contentious proposal, but its value-for-money and equity impact depend entirely on whether generalist salary reforms reach specialists and whether enrollment growth reflects real future supply rather than non-practicing parent-trainees.
